sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.92 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: For a field$K$of characteristic 2, each finite group$G=\{ g_1, ,g_n\}$of odd order$n$is determined up to isomorphism by its group determinant (Formanek--Sibley, 1990) and even by its reduced norm\/ which is defined as the last coefficient$s_m(x)$of the minimal polynomial$( ;x)= ^m - s_1(x) ^{m-1} + + (- 1)^{m}s_m(x)$for the generic element$x=x_1g_1 + + x_ng_n$of the group ring$KG$(Hoehnke, 1991). ...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
